message:
feat(freeze): tiered freeze-all with thin codegen for 10,828 functions

Add two-tier frozen codegen: top 200 functions keep full inline codegen,
remaining 10,628 get thin codegen that eliminates interpreter dispatch
with minimal code size. Key components:

- ThinCodeGen (new): switch-in-loop state machine, one helper call per
  opcode, no vstack optimization. Resolves bytecode atom IDs to runtime
  atoms via JS_NewAtom to avoid module-local atom mismatch.

- thin runtime helpers (zig_runtime.thin.*): stack-direct noinline
  helpers for op_call, op_get_field_ic, op_put_field, exceptionCleanup
  and inline helpers for push/pop/get_loc/put_loc operations.

- Tier selection in frozen_registry: sort by profile score, top N get
  full/relooper codegen, rest get thin codegen.

- Fix @branchHint(.cold) placement: move from emitExceptionReturn() to
  conditional callers only (Zig 0.15 requires it as first statement in
  conditional branch, not unconditional .throw blocks).

- Clean up all debug fprintf instrumentation from quickjs.c patch.

Result: 249.6MB binary, all 10,828 functions frozen, TSC compiles
TypeScript successfully at ~6.5s (vs 4-5s N=200-only baseline).
